title: Cronquistianthus volkensii
taxonomy: Cronquistianthus volkensii is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. The genus Cronquistianthus is named after Arthur Cronquist, a renowned American botanist. Cronquistianthus volkensii is a species of flowering plant endemic to East Africa. It belongs to the tribe Mutisieae, a group of plants that are often characterized by their small, daisy-like flowers. Cronquistianthus volkensii is also sometimes referred to as a member of the infrageneric classification of Mutisia.

intro: Cronquistianthus volkensii, also known as Volkens' bitter lettuce, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contributing to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Cronquistianthus volkensii is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
